  "decision": "fail",

  "percent_complete": 100,

  "confidence": 1.0,
  "event_time": 0.0,
  "rate": 0.0,
  "order": [],
  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "DP impulse lines sloped incorrectly for the service type",
    "Gas taps installed below the pipe centerline, risking liquid accumulation in the lines",
    "Liquid taps installed on top of the pipe, risking gas/air pocket entrapment in the lines"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Isolate the transmitter and impulse lines from the process",
    "Drain/vent the impulse lines to safe disposal",
    "Re-route and re-slope the impulse lines according to service type (gas taps on top/side, liquid taps on side/bottom)",
    "Re-commission, leak test, and zero-check the transmitter"
  ],
  "rationale": "The differential pressure (DP) transmitter impulse lines are sloped incorrectly for the process fluid. For gas service, taps must be on top of the pipe to allow condensate to drain back. For liquid service, taps must be on the side or bottom to prevent gas bubbles from being trapped. This incorrect installation leads to measurement errors, sluggish response, or complete loss of signal due to liquid/gas pockets.",
  "references": [
    "API RP 551 (Process Measurement Instrumentation)",
    "Manufacturer Installation Manuals (DP Transmitter Impulse Piping Guidelines)"
  ]
}
